# inkwell

> A local-first markdown editor for macOS, Windows, and Linux. Notes are stored as plain .md files in a folder you choose — no accounts, no cloud, no telemetry.

inkwell is free and open source, built with Tauri, React, and Rust.

Key features: live split-view markdown preview, eight built-in themes, a full drawing canvas (flowcharts, mind maps, sequence diagrams), Kanban boards, GitHub sync for notes, a Claude MCP server for reading/writing the vault from Claude, a global Quick Note capture window, and a weekly planner with tagged tasks.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://inkwell-lp.vercel.app/): overview, features, and download links for macOS, Windows, and Linux
- [Documentation](https://inkwell-lp.vercel.app/docs): install instructions per platform, editor and canvas usage, themes, export, GitHub sync, Claude MCP setup, and keyboard shortcuts
- [Privacy](https://inkwell-lp.vercel.app/privacy): what the app collects (nothing) and what the website collects (anonymous page-view analytics)

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/coros-hq/inkwell)
- [Latest release](https://github.com/coros-hq/inkwell/releases/latest)
